streams.reasoning: From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: electrophile stream kept separate to ensure controlled mixing and avoid premature reaction with pre-activated nucleophile
From Chemistry Plan stream_logic: nucleophile + catalyst stream where piperidine can pre-activate ethyl cyanoacetate forming reactive enolate/enamine species before mixing with aldehyde

pre_council_proposal.mixing_order_reasoning: From Chemistry Plan: Stream A (benzaldehyde) and Stream B (ethyl cyanoacetate + piperidine) combined at T-mixer at equal flow rates. Pre-mixing piperidine with ethyl cyanoacetate allows partial enolization/enamine formation before contact with aldehyde, ensuring efficient nucleophilic addition upon mixing.
pre_council_proposal.chemistry_notes: Thermal organocatalytic Knoevenagel condensation driven by piperidine-catalyzed enolization and dehydration. Rate-limiting step is nucleophilic addition of activated methylene to benzaldehyde carbonyl. Flow advantage: enhanced mixing reduces 8h batch time to ~20min residence time at elevated temperature under back-pressure. Water elimination is thermodynamically favorable and product is E-selective. No oxygen/moisture sensitivity allows air atmosphere operation.

reasoning_per_field.residence_time_min: Analogy 2 used 20 min for condensation reaction with piperidine base at elevated temperature. Calculation estimated 187.8 min but analogies show much faster kinetics possible. Using 20 min from most similar thermal condensation analogy.
reasoning_per_field.flow_rate_mL_min: Total flow = 0.5 mL/min from equal streams of 0.25 mL/min each. Gives residence time = 10 mL / 0.5 mL/min = 20 min matching analogy.
reasoning_per_field.temperature_C: Increased from batch 80°C to 100°C. Chemistry Plan suggests 100°C. Analogy 2 used 120°C for condensation - using 100°C as intermediate value with BPR to superheat ethanol.
reasoning_per_field.concentration_M: Final concentration 0.25 M after 1:1 mixing of 0.5 M streams. Analogy 1 used 0.25 M successfully for Knoevenagel condensation.
reasoning_per_field.BPR_bar: Calculation shows BPR required at 5.0 bar. Ethanol bp=78°C, operating at 100°C requires pressure. Analogy 2 used 9.7 bar - using 5.0 bar as sufficient.
reasoning_per_field.reactor_type: Coil reactor specified in Chemistry Plan. Standard for thermal reactions.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_material: PFA chosen for high temperature thermal reaction compatibility up to 260°C. Analogy 2 used PFA successfully.
reasoning_per_field.tubing_ID_mm: 1.0 mm from calculations for good mixing and reasonable pressure drop. Standard for thermal flow chemistry.
reasoning_per_field.reactor_volume_mL: 10 mL volume from Analogy 2 which had similar thermal condensation process. Gives desired residence time with calculated flow rate.
reasoning_per_field.light_setup: Empty string - no photochemistry involved per Chemistry Plan. Purely thermal process.
reasoning_per_field.wavelength_nm: null - no light required for thermal organocatalytic process.

steps.summary: t_mix = 259.4 s, Da = 21.721 — Mass-transfer limited — mixing is the bottleneck

values.interpretation: Mass-transfer limited — mixing is the bottleneck

warnings: Da = 21.7 ≫ 1: severely mass-transfer limited. Need micromixer, smaller d, or pre-mixed streams.

steps.name: Heat Transfer
steps.summary: Q_gen = 0.0848 W, Q_rem = 150.7934 W, Da_th = 0.0006 — Thermally safe

steps.name: Back-Pressure Regulator
steps.summary: BPR REQUIRED: 5.0 bar (Gas-liquid system detected — BPR mandatory to maintain gas solubility (Henry's law: C_gas = H·P) and prevent uncontrolled degassing.)
equations: C_{\mathrm{gas}} = H \cdot P_{\mathrm{partial}}\quad\text{(higher BPR} \Rightarrow \text{more dissolved gas)}
